**WSO2 Tooling Architecture Revamp**

**1\. Existing issues**

* Ownership is spread across several repos with different boundaries.  
* vscode-extensions contains Ballerina, MI, common UI components and fonts, while SI is separate.  
* WSO2 Integrator extension sits inside the product-integrator, and current welcome / project creation is centralized there.  
* A single end-to-end change often crosses multiple repos. (This creates context switching and makes agentic development harder)

**2\. Objectives**

* Reduce cross-repo coordination for common feature changes.  
* Clarify ownership between product logic, shared foundation and product shell.  
* Make each product easier for AI agents to understand and modify in one working context.

**3\. Current structure**

![][image1]

*Figure 1\. Current repository structure*

**4\. Proposed structure**

![][image2]

*Figure 2\. Proposed repository structure*

* Each product repo should own its extension, welcome page, project creation, product workflows and language server.  
* Keep product-integrator as the customised VS Code / desktop shell only.  
* Keep only a small shared foundation repo for stable cross-product assets.

**6\. Ownership split**

| Layer | Owns |
| :---- | :---- |
| Product repos | Welcome page, project creation, product UX, language server, product workflows |
| Shared foundation *Currently: wso2/vscode-extensions* | Tokens, icons, fonts, stable helpers / contracts |
| product-integrator | Desktop shell, global config, runtime management, bundling |

**7\. Why this is agent-friendly**

* One repo can contain one end-to-end product change.  
* AI agents can complete UI \+ extension \+ language-server work with less repo hopping.  
* The product shell becomes simpler and more deterministic.  
* The shared layer stays small, reducing hidden dependencies.

**8\. Steps**

- [x] ~~Create one main repo per product: ballerina-tooling, mi-tooling and si-tooling.~~  
- [x] ~~Merge ballerina-grammar into ballerina-tooling unless strong external reuse requires a separate repo.~~  
- [ ] Confirm final repo boundaries and naming.  
- [ ] Move welcome / project creation from product-integrator into each product repo.  
- [ ] Move configuration pages into product-integrator.  
- [ ] Finalize and implement CI/CD \+ release pipelines
